how_to_reproduce: docker compose up -d postgres

load the committed corpus into postgres via rag_tools.ingest.load_parquet against indexes/corpus-0.1.1-bge-m3-03f983e0, the same idempotent load testing/tests/test_pgvector_adapter_live.py's own ensure_chunks_loaded fixture performs
docker compose --profile observability up -d otel-collector phoenix
set -a; source .env.fastlane; set +a   # ATLAS_TEI_EMBED_URL/ATLAS_TEI_RERANK_URL -> the real amd64 fastlane node, skipping the ~45min local Rosetta TEI warmup

a standalone operator script (not part of the committed test suite) constructs a REAL OtelTracer (opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.http.trace_exporter.OTLPSpanExporter, endpoint http://localhost:4318, the compose otel-collector's own published OTLP HTTP port) and a REAL PgvectorRetriever (postgresql://atlas:atlas-dev-password@localhost:5433/atlas, the compose postgres service's own published port, plus the fastlane TEI endpoints above), then drives three scenarios through the REAL /chat/stream ASGI edge (atlas.chat_app.make_chat_app), in process (httpx's ASGITransport, no separate backend container needed -- the SAME real OTLP wire export a container would produce), replay mode, ATLAS_CHECKPOINTER=inmemory

round 2 (the model's answer AFTER seeing real retrieved passages) cassette content is never hand guessed: atlas.orchestration.atlas_graph._knowledge_call(retriever, query) is called directly first, against the SAME real retriever, to obtain the exact ToolMessage content a real turn would see, which then seeds the cassette
POST /auth/login {"customer_id": "cust_current"} -> access_token, once per scenario (a fresh app/tracer/cassette dir each time)

POST /chat/stream (SSE) with the access token, three scenarios: (a) thread freeze-rag-1p1, 'What is a data cap?' against the real (good password) retriever -- real embed/retrieve/rerank/assemble stage spans, the search_knowledge tool span, a full completed turn closing atlas.stage.ttft_ms; (b) thread freeze-bindguard-1p1, 'What plans do you offer?', a cassette proposing list_plans (a CATALOG tool, unreachable under the real classify_intent() heuristic, since the live edge only ever returns 'troubleshooting' for non action text, backend/atlas/domain/binding.py, which binds KNOWLEDGE_TOOLS | READ_TOOLS, no catalog tools) -- tools_read's own bind_guard GUARDRAIL span (atlas.guard.decision=block) plus a full streamed safe handoff answer, closing ttft; (c) thread freeze-degraded-1p1, 'What is a data cap?' against a SEPARATE retriever instance pointed at an intentionally wrong postgres password -- every retrieval attempt fails, refusal_trigger fires, the terminal refusal node span (atlas.degradation.mode=refusal), closing ttft
docker exec atlas-otel-collector-1 -> /var/log/otel/raw-otlp-archive.jsonl -> this file's 'exports' array (one parsed JSON object per JSONL line)

scenarios.backend: healthy (real postgres dsn, real fastlane TEI)
scenarios.outcome: real RAG stages + search_knowledge tool span, full completed turn, closes ttft
scenarios.question: What is a data cap?

scenarios.backend: healthy (real postgres dsn, real fastlane TEI)
scenarios.outcome: bind_guard GUARDRAIL span (block, list_plans unreachable under troubleshooting) + full streamed safe handoff answer, closes ttft
scenarios.question: What plans do you offer?

scenarios.outcome: refusal_trigger + refusal node span (atlas.degradation.mode=refusal), closes ttft
scenarios.question: What is a data cap?

_capture.what: I1 fix (SP6 final review): the raw OTLP archive the otel-collector's own file/raw_archive exporter wrote (infra/observability/otel-collector.yaml) during one live capture window against the docker compose observability profile, with a real OtelTracer (OTLP HTTP export) and the fastlane TEI node (.env.fastlane), REPLACING the SP6 task 7 capture now that OtelTracer.open() stamps atlas.turn.seq on every span (trace 1.1.0). Every span here is REAL and REDACTED (the SAME redaction the collector applies before either fan out target, Phoenix or this archive, ever sees a span -- see the redaction.redacted.keys / redaction.redacted.count diagnostic attributes the collector itself adds, visible on several spans below). This is the freeze's own evidence source (b); contract_tools.freeze_check.live_emitted_attributes reads it. Attribute key coverage checked against the prior capture (23 keys): all 23 still present, plus exactly one new key, atlas.turn.seq -- nothing regressed.
_capture.git_sha_note: the capture ran on the mid fix wave working tree (dirty relative to 8522043, which cannot itself produce atlas.turn.seq); span content is validated by the freeze_check 30 of 30 gate, which reads attribute keys, and by the bidirectional join test

sp8_task1_addendum.why: trace 1.1.0 to 1.2.0: the four attributes ADR-029 narrowed for SP8 (atlas.judge.id, atlas.judge.rubric_version, atlas.judge.verdict, atlas.subject.pseudonym) now have real emitters (testing/harness/judge/emission.py, backend/atlas/adapters/trace_translation.py's pseudonym_for). This addendum's 3 exports are APPENDED to the original 45 above, which are untouched and still valid evidence for every attribute they already proved.
how_to_reproduce: docker compose --profile observability up -d otel-collector   # postgres/phoenix/otel-archive-init come up as its dependencies; no TEI/fastlane needed for this capture

a standalone operator script (not part of the committed test suite) constructs a REAL OtelTracer (opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.http.trace_exporter.OTLPSpanExporter, endpoint http://localhost:4318, the compose otel-collector's own published OTLP HTTP port)

tracer.open('turn', 'turn', input=..., intent='troubleshooting', customer_id='cust_sp8_live_capture_001') -- a real turn span with a real customer_id, proving atlas.subject.pseudonym (the redacted span carries the HMAC, never the raw id or the input/intent passthrough keys, both stripped by the SAME redaction processor every other capture already goes through)

judge.emission.emit_verdict(tracer, root, JudgeContract('gpt-judge-2026-06-01', 'groundedness-v1', <hash>), 'grounded') then again with 'ungrounded' -- two judge_verdict spans, proving atlas.judge.id/atlas.judge.rubric_version/atlas.judge.verdict on both wire enum values
tracer._provider.force_flush() -- SimpleSpanProcessor already exports synchronously per span.end(), this just waits for the in flight export before the process exits

docker cp atlas-otel-collector-1:/var/log/otel/raw-otlp-archive.jsonl -> this addendum's own 3 export lines (the collector image ships neither sh nor cat, so docker cp reads the file directly rather than a docker exec cat, unlike the original capture's own step)

sp9_task5_addendum.why: trace 1.2.0 to 1.3.0: the usage accounting trio ADR-029 narrowed since v0.1.0 (atlas.cost.input_tokens, atlas.cost.output_tokens, atlas.cost.usd) now has a real emitter (testing/harness/matrix/cost_emission.py's emit_cost, a new kind="llm" span, generator_cost, never wired into the live graph -- the same D29 batch, report time disposition judge/emission.py's own module docstring already establishes). This addendum's 5 exports are APPENDED to the 48 above (45 original + the SP8 task 1 addendum's own 3), which are untouched and still valid evidence for every attribute they already proved.
how_to_reproduce: docker compose --profile observability up -d otel-collector   # postgres/phoenix/otel-archive-init come up as its dependencies; no TEI/fastlane needed for this capture

a standalone operator script (not part of the committed test suite) constructs a REAL OtelTracer (opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.http.trace_exporter.OTLPSpanExporter, endpoint http://localhost:4318, the compose otel-collector's own published OTLP HTTP port)
tracer.open('turn', 'turn', input=..., intent='troubleshooting', customer_id='cust_sp9_live_capture_001') -- a real turn span, the SAME parent nesting pattern the SP8 addendum already proved

matrix.cost_emission.emit_cost(tracer, root, model_id=..., input_tokens=..., output_tokens=..., usd=matrix.spend_gate.cost_from_usage(provider, usage)) three times (anthropic:claude-sonnet-5, openai:gpt-5.6-sol, ollama:qwen2.5:7b) -- three generator_cost spans, proving atlas.cost.input_tokens/output_tokens/usd, including a real, present $0.0 for the Ollama arm that always runs free (never omitted, never 'unavailable')

judge.emission.emit_verdict(tracer, root, JudgeContract('gpt-judge-2026-06-01', 'groundedness-v1', <hash>), 'grounded') alongside, proving the judge trio already closed still emits correctly in the same session (belt and suspenders, not required by this task)
tracer._provider.force_flush() -- SimpleSpanProcessor already exports synchronously per span.end(), this just waits for the in flight export before the process exits

docker cp atlas-otel-collector-1:/var/log/otel/raw-otlp-archive.jsonl -> this addendum's own 5 export lines (the collector image ships neither sh nor cat, so docker cp reads the file directly rather than a docker exec cat)
